potential_learnings.title: JSON-LD extraction must use HTML source parsing when CDP execute_script throws SyntaxError
potential_learnings.description: Some sites with Content Security Policy (CSP) restrictions or specific DOM structures cause driver.execute_script() to throw SyntaxError when trying to access JSON-LD blocks via CDP. The workaround is to extract JSON-LD by parsing driver.page_source with regex or BeautifulSoup instead of using JavaScript evaluation. This was observed on Bulgari.com (LVMH) where CDP evaluation failed but HTML source parsing worked correctly.
evidence: product_analysis.json: 'previous scraper logs show SyntaxError in JavaScript evaluation for JSON-LD extraction'
product_analysis.json critical_recommendations: 'Do NOT use driver.execute_script() for JSON-LD extraction. Use driver.page_source and BeautifulSoup/re'
scraper.py uses extract_jsonld_from_html() with regex on page_source — confirmed working
potential_learnings.proposed_change: Add a new section to jsonld-extraction SKILL.md: 'CDP Evaluation Fallback' explaining that when driver.execute_script() fails (SyntaxError, CSP block), fall back to parsing page_source HTML with regex/BeautifulSoup for <script type='application/ld+json'> tags. Include the regex pattern and the Python function from the scraper.

potential_learnings.title: SeleniumBase UC Mode as preferred UC Chrome wrapper with reconnect resilience
potential_learnings.description: The Bulgari scraper uses SeleniumBase SB(uc=True) instead of raw undetected_chromedriver. SeleniumBase provides uc_open_with_reconnect() which automatically handles ChromeDriver connection drops (observed in test run attempt 1: 'Connection refused' error). It also provides built-in Xvfb support, simpler initialization, and auto-version detection. For sites requiring UC Chrome, SeleniumBase UC Mode offers better resilience than raw undetected_chromedriver.
evidence: scraper.py: uses 'from seleniumbase import SB' with 'sb = SB(uc=True)' and 'driver.uc_open_with_reconnect(url, reconnect_time=4)'
test_report.json run_history attempt 1: 'ChromeDriver connection lost during warmup - Connection refused error'
test_report.json run_history attempt 3: SUCCESS with reconnect mechanism
potential_learnings.proposed_change: Add a subsection to anti-bot-handling SKILL.md under 'Stealth Browser Patterns' covering SeleniumBase UC Mode as an alternative to raw undetected_chromedriver. Highlight uc_open_with_reconnect() for handling transient ChromeDriver connection drops, built-in Xvfb support, and simpler API.

potential_learnings.title: LVMH luxury brand site patterns: TLS fingerprinting, locale URLs, hidden pricing
potential_learnings.description: LVMH luxury brand sites (Bulgari, Dior, Louis Vuitton, Tiffany, Sephora) share common characteristics: (1) Custom enterprise ecommerce platforms (not Shopify/SFCC), (2) TLS/browser fingerprinting that blocks standard Playwright and HTTP clients, (3) Require UC Chrome with no proxy needed, (4) Locale-prefixed URLs (/en-us/, /en-int/), (5) JSON-LD with potentially empty offers.price (luxury brands often hide pricing), (6) data-testid attributes on key elements, (7) Cookie consent walls using Onetrust or custom banners, (8) Soft 404 pages that redirect to category listing.
evidence: site_analysis.json: 'Bulgari.com is a luxury LVMH brand site that requires UC Chrome'
product_analysis.json: 'Site is a custom LVMH ecommerce platform. Confirmed as not Shopify, SFCC, or any standard platform'
product_analysis.json: 'Price may not be in JSON-LD (common for luxury brands that hide pricing)'
potential_learnings.proposed_change: Create a new skill 'lvmh-detection' covering LVMH brand detection (LVMH portfolio includes ~75 brands), shared platform characteristics, UC Chrome requirement, locale URL patterns, data-testid conventions, cookie consent handling (Onetrust), and extraction patterns for luxury brand pages.

analysis_notes: The scrape was infrastructure-valid (UC Chrome connectivity, warmup, cookie consent, soft 404 detection all worked) but could not validate field extraction because all input URLs were dead (soft 404s). The most valuable learnings are: (1) the JSON-LD CDP-vs-HTML-source extraction pattern, (2) SeleniumBase UC Mode with reconnect, and (3) LVMH brand patterns. No navigation learnings were auto-applied (no nav_learning_report.json).
